word_list.word: a little nonsense now and then, is cherished by the wisest men
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈɪɾəl nˈɑːnsəns nˈaʊ ænd ðˈɛn ɪz tʃˈɛɹɪʃt baɪ ðə wˈaɪsəst mˈɛn

senses.definition: used to imply that taking a break from serious matters to enjoy some playfulness can be beneficial and even wise

examples.x: The CEO held occasional company events that were lighthearted and fun, as he believed that a little nonsense now and then is cherished by the wisest men, and that it helped to foster a positive work culture.

word_list.word: a little of what you fancy does you good
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈɪɾəl ʌv wˌʌt juː fˈænsi dˈʌz juː ɡˈʊd

senses.definition: used to suggest that indulging in small amounts of something enjoyable or desired can have positive effects on one's well-being, emphasizing the value of moderation

word_list.word: better a little fire to warm us than a great one to burn us
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚɹ ɐ lˈɪɾəl fˈaɪɚ tə wˈɔːɹm ˌʌs ðˌænə ɡɹˈeɪt wˈʌn tə bˈɜːn ˌʌs

senses.definition: used to imply that it is better to have a modest or moderate amount of something than to have too much of it, which may lead to negative consequences or harm
used to mean that it is better to take action or complete a task, even if it is delayed, rather than not doing it at all

examples.x: I know I missed your birthday, but here's your gift now, better late than never.
It took a while, but I’m finally on board with this plan—better late than never.
I finally made it to your wedding, better late than never!

word_list.word: eat at pleasure, drink with measure
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːt æt plˈɛʒɚ dɹˈɪŋk wɪð mˈɛʒɚ

senses.definition: used to advise that it is acceptable to indulge in eating to one's satisfaction, but it is important to control alcohol intake to avoid harmful effects on one's health

word_list.word: enough is as good as a feast
word_list.pronunciation: ɪnˈʌf ɪz æz ɡˈʊd æz ɐ fˈiːst

senses.definition: used to suggest that having just enough of something is preferable to having too much of it, emphasizing the value of moderation and contentment over excess

examples.x: The company decided to focus on producing quality products rather than expanding rapidly and taking on too much, as they understood that enough is as good as a feast.

word_list.word: the half is better than the whole
word_list.pronunciation: ðə hˈæf ɪz bˈɛɾɚ ðɐn ðə hˈoʊl

senses.definition: used to suggest that having a smaller portion of something can be more beneficial or valuable and less overwhelming or burdensome than having the entire thing

examples.x: When cleaning your house, it's often better to do a little bit each day, rather than trying to clean everything at once and becoming overwhelmed, remembering that the half is better than the whole.

word_list.word: honey in excess is no longer sweet
word_list.pronunciation: hˈʌni ɪn ɛksˈɛs ɪz nˌoʊ lˈɑːŋɡɚ swˈiːt

senses.definition: used to imply that even the most pleasurable or enjoyable things can lose their appeal if they are overindulged in or taken to extremes

examples.x: The athlete knew that overtraining could lead to burnout and injury, so she balanced intense workouts with rest and recovery, as she believed that honey in excess is no longer sweet.

word_list.word: it is part of a good shepherd to shear his flock, not (to|) skin it
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz pˈɑːɹt əvə ɡˈʊd ʃˈɛpɚd tə ʃˈɪɹ hɪz flˈɑːk nˌɑːt tʊ skˈɪn ɪt

senses.definition: used to emphasize that a good leader takes care of those they are responsible for, by taking only what is necessary and not exploiting or harming them

word_list.word: keep no more cats than can catch mice
word_list.pronunciation: kˈiːp nˈoʊmˌoːɹ kˈæts ðɐn kæn kˈætʃ mˈaɪs

senses.definition: used to suggest that one should not keep more resources, tools, or employees than are necessary to accomplish a task or objective, as it can be wasteful and inefficient

examples.x: The manager only hired the number of employees necessary to complete the project, as she believed in the saying keep no more cats than can catch mice.

word_list.word: there is measure in all things
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz mˈɛʒɚɹ ɪn ˈɔːl θˈɪŋz

senses.definition: used to emphasize that moderation and balance are important in all aspects of life

examples.x: In managing a team, it's important to remember that there is measure in all things, and to strike a balance between delegation and micromanagement.

word_list.word: eat to live, not live to eat
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːt tə lˈɪv nˌɑːt lˈaɪv tʊ ˈiːt

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of moderation in eating and treating food as fuel for the body rather than a source of constant pleasure or comfort

word_list.word: little and often fills the purse
word_list.pronunciation: lˈɪɾəl ænd ˈɔfən fˈɪlz ðə pˈɜːs

senses.definition: used to advise that consistent, small efforts can lead to significant results over time, as regular small savings can accumulate to create a considerable amount of wealth

examples.x: The athlete trained for short periods of time several times a week, as she believed that little and often fills the purse and that consistent effort would help her achieve her goals.

word_list.word: life is hard by the yard, but by the inch life is a cinch
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪf ɪz hˈɑːɹd baɪ ðə jˈɑːɹd bˌʌt baɪ ðɪ ˈɪntʃ lˈaɪf ɪz ɐ sˈɪntʃ

senses.definition: used to suggest that when faced with difficult tasks or challenges, it is easier to tackle them in small, manageable steps rather than trying to handle everything at once
used to imply that excess or deficiency in any area can lead to negative consequences, and that finding a proper balance is the key to a healthy and fulfilling life

examples.x: In consuming media and entertainment, it's important to remember to practice moderation in all things, to avoid becoming too consumed or addicted.

word_list.word: meat and mass never hindered man
word_list.pronunciation: mˈiːt ænd mˈæs nˈɛvɚ hˈɪndɚd mˈæn

senses.definition: used to imply that it is possible to find a balance between satisfying material needs and nurturing one's spiritual or emotional well-being

word_list.word: all work and no play makes Jack (become|) a dull boy
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɔːl wˈɜːk ænd nˈoʊ plˈeɪ mˌeɪks dʒˈæk bɪkˌʌm ɐ dˈʌl bˈɔɪ

senses.definition: used to suggest that if someone only focuses on work and does not take time for leisure activities or enjoyment, they will become boring, uninteresting, and unhappy

examples.x: You need a break from your studies—after all, all work and no play makes Jack a dull boy.
He’s been working nonstop lately, but remember, all work and no play makes Jack a dull boy.
I told her to take a day off; all work and no play makes Jack a dull boy.
All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy, so they decided to go on a weekend trip.
Don’t forget to take care of yourself; all work and no play makes Jack a dull boy in the long run.
